properties.LocationArea: SH 73 Yaldhurst, near Kirk Road intersection
properties.EventComments: Due to a crash, State Highway 73 is closed at Kirk Road intersection. Road users are advised to follow the detour and allow extra time for travel.
properties.AlternativeRoute: Westbound traffic turn right onto Buchanans Road, then left onto Old West Coast Road, left onto Chattertons Road, then right back onto State Highway 73. Reverse for eastbound traffic.
properties.Name: Road Closure: SH 73 Yaldhurst, near Kirk Road intersection

properties.LocationArea: SH 1 Waikuku

properties.LocationArea: SH 1 Terrace Tunnel, Wellington
properties.EventComments: Due to maintenance works, the Terrace Tunnel is scheduled to close northbound tonight from 9pm to 4am. Please note southbound will remain unaffected. These works are subject to change and are dependent on various factors.
properties.AlternativeRoute: For Northbound traffic use Willis Street onto Ghuznee Street onto the Terrace onto Everton Terrace onto Clifton Onramp. Please note this detour will add approximately 5 minutes to your journey times. Road users are advised to plan ahead.
properties.Name: Road Closure: SH 1 Terrace Tunnel, Wellington

properties.LocationArea: SH 1 Ohau, between McLeavy Road and Bishops Road

properties.LocationArea: SH 2 Napier, between the intersections with Meeanee Road and Taradale Road
properties.EventComments: Due to traffic switch works, this road will be closed in both directions on Sunday 19th July and Monday 20th July nights between 7pm and 6am. Plan your journey accordingly allowing extra time for travel.
properties.AlternativeRoute:
Light vehicles, detour via Meeanee Road, Lee Road, Gloucester Street, Kennedy Road and  Prebensen Drive. 
Heavy vehicles, detour via Meeanee Road, Awatoto Road and State Highway 51.
properties.Name: Road Closure: SH 2 Napier, between the intersections with Meeanee Road and Taradale Road

properties.LocationArea: SH 1 Greville Rd On-ramp, Southbound

properties.LocationArea: SH 88 Dunedin, between the intersection of Cumberland Street and Castle Street
properties.EventComments: Due to hospital upgrade works, the road will be closed in both directions between 6am and 3pm daily from Tuesday 7th July to Friday 31st July. Outside of these hours there will be a lane shift in place. Plan your journey accordingly allowing extra time for travel. Please note due to public holidays, staff resourcing and other factors, closures may not be utilized.
properties.AlternativeRoute: Follow sign posted detour on site.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 88 Dunedin, between the intersection of Cumberland Street and Castle Street

properties.LocationArea: SH 94 Te Anau to Milford, at the Homer Tunnel
properties.EventComments: Please drive with care. Homer Tunnel maintenance works are scheduled on the nights of Monday to Wednesday (July 20th - 22nd). The tunnel will be closed from 6.30pm to 2am, with a temporary opening at 9pm to allow waiting traffic through. Any queries, please call 027 202 3467 (as we're currently experiencing issues with our landline after-hours).
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 94 Te Anau to Milford, at the Homer Tunnel

properties.LocationArea: SH 1 Kaikoura to Cheviot
properties.EventComments:
State Highway 1 between Kaikoura and Cheviot has re-opened following an earlier road closure due to flooding and road damage.
The road is open to one lane near Hawkswood under stop/go temporary traffic management. Expect delays along this route and allow for extra time to your journey.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 1 Kaikoura to Cheviot

properties.LocationArea: SH 1 Waikato Expressway, Cambridge section
properties.EventComments: Due to road resurfacing, SH1 Waikato Expressway, (Cambridge) will be one lane each direction from the Southern Interchange/Tirau Rd to Victoria Rd Interchange at Hautapu from Sunday 1 March. Work then progresses in 5km sections north to Tamahere. Lanes closed 24/7; expect delays at peak times. Allow extra travel time and travel with care through the worksites. Works run until November.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 1 Waikato Expressway, Cambridge section

properties.LocationArea: SH 2 Kaitoke to Featherston (Remutaka Hill)
properties.EventComments: Due to various works on Remutaka Hill, the road is scheduled to close in both directions on Sunday 19th July night between the hours of 9pm and 4am. Plan your journey accordingly avoiding travel during these times. Bookings are no longer required for crossing over the hill during these closures. Please visit www.nzta.govt.nz/remutaka for crossing times and arrive 15-minutes prior to scheduled crossings.
properties.AlternativeRoute: Northbound traffic detour via SH1 to Ohau, then turn right onto SH57, continue along SH57 and then turn right onto SH3, continue along SH3 until you rejoin SH2 in Woodville. Reverse for southbound. Please note: this detour route will add approximately three and a half hours to your journey time.
properties.Name: Area Warning: SH 2 Kaitoke to Featherston (Remutaka Hill)

properties.LocationArea: SH 1 Levin to Foxton at Waitarere Beach Road

properties.LocationArea: SH 8 Raes Junction to Milton

properties.LocationArea: SH 2 Papamoa, near intersection of The Sands Avenue
properties.EventComments:
Due to construction works, the Northbound The Sands Avenue onramp will be closed on 22 Jul 2026 to 06 Nov 2026 between 2000 and 0400.

Road users are advised to plan their journey accordingly allowing extra time for travel.

Please note that these works are weather dependent and may be postponed at short notice.
properties.AlternativeRoute: Southbound road users on The Sands Avenue will enter SH2 via Southbound Tauranga Eastern Link onramp, then will exit SH2 via offramp to Te Ara A Tia Road, then right turn on to Te Ara A Tia Road, then U-turn on to Kopura Road/Te Ara A Tia Road RAB, then rejoin SH2 via the onramp.
properties.Name: Road Closure: SH 2 Papamoa, near intersection of The Sands Avenue

properties.LocationArea: SH 2 Papamoa, near intersection of The Sands Avenue
properties.EventComments:
Due to construction works, the Southbound The Sands Avenue offramp will be closed on 22 Jul 2026 to 23 Jul 2026 between 2000 and 0400.

Road users are advised to plan their journey accordingly allowing extra time for travel.

Please note that these works are weather dependent and may be postponed at short notice.
properties.AlternativeRoute: Southbound road users will exit SH2 via offramp to Te Ara A Tia Road, then right turn on to Te Ara A Tia Road, then U-turn on to Kopura Road/Te Ara A Tia Road RAB, then rejoin SH2 via the onramp, and continue SH2 up to Tauranga Eastern Link offramp back to The Sands Avenue.
properties.Name: Road Closure: SH 2 Papamoa, near intersection of The Sands Avenue

properties.EventComments: From Wednesday 29 April, traffic will be reduced to one lane about 1km north of the SH36/Hamurana Road intersection, while a culvert is replaced. This will include traffic signals, stop/go signage, lane and shoulder closures, and a temporary speed limit of 30km/h. Lane restrictions are expected to remain in place until construction is complete in early 2027.

Oversized vehicles intending to use SH36 are required to detour via SH2, SH33, SH30 and SH5 for travel between Rotorua and Tauranga.

properties.Name: News: South Island Regional and District Council Websites

properties.LocationArea: Kuaotunu River Bridge Repairs
properties.Content:
FINAL UPDATE THU 17 OCT 2019

Kuaotunu River bridge reopens

Our contractors have done a fantastic job to get the bridge opened ahead of schedule, and more than a week before Labour weekend, despite the heavy rain and strong winds that battered the area earlier this week. Traffic management remains in place, including a 30km/h speed restriction.

Please note that, weather dependent, the bridge will need to close again for the day on Monday (21 Oct) to carry out resurfacing works.

---------------

 

UPDATE TUE 08 OCT 2019

Repairing Kuaotunu bridge to reconnect SH25 

Work to repair the Kuaotunu Bridge on SH25 Coromandel after last month’s storm is progressing well, with the bridge expected to reopen within the next couple of weeks.

The repair team has finished driving the piles on the northern side of the bridge and has switched to the southern side. Once all the piles have been driven they will need to be cut to size and the bridge abutments filled with concrete before work to reinstate the bridge deck can begin.

“We understand that these works are frustrating for local residents and those who travel to the area often, however, our contractors are working as hard as they can to repair the damage as quickly and efficiently as possible,” says Transport Agency Waikato Systems Manager Karen Boyt.  

The Transport Agency has joined together with Thames Coromandel District Council to provide a free shuttle bus service from Kuaotunu to Whitianga every day while the bridge is closed.

The Go Kiwi shuttle departs from outside the Kuaotunu fire station, at 7:30am and 8:30am and then returns from Whitianga at 4:30pm and 5:30pm. From Wednesday 9 October there will also be a lunchtime bus leaving Whitianga at 11.30 and Kuaotunu at 12pm. 

Anyone travelling in the area should check our Journey Planner, www.journeys.nzta.govt.nz or call 0800 44 44 49 before leaving for the most up to date information. 

[image src="/assets/8e66b60fd1/NZTA-3.jpg" id="1023" width="657" height="507" class="leftAlone ss-htmleditorfield-file image" title="NZTA 3"]

[image src="/assets/5b38db22be/nzta-2.jpg" id="1022" width="657" height="586" class="leftAlone ss-htmleditorfield-file image" title="nzta 2"]

 

 ---------------

Following an assessment after recent heavy rain, the SH25 Kuaotunu River bridge has been closed, for around five weeks, to repair weather damage.

We understand the frustration this may cause for those who live in or travel to the area often, however our contractors are working as hard as they can to repair the damage as quickly and efficiently as possible.

During this time detours will be clearly signposted via either SH25A and SH25 or SH25 Thames Coast Road.

 [image src="/assets/dd1a4446a4/Kuaotunu-IMG_STOP.jpg" id="1019" width="251" height="303" class="ss-htmleditorfield-file image right" title="Kuaotunu IMG STOP"]
  • Residents south of Blackjack Road will need to access Kuaotunu from Whitianga.
  • Residents west of the bridge will only have vehicle access from the Coromandel side.
  • Pedestrians will be able to cross the river via a separate, existing walk bridge. 

We hope to have the bridge open again by Labour Weekend, however, this is weather dependant. We will keep the community updated, and thank you for your patience during this time.

 

UPDATE TUE 24 SEP 2019

Repair works to SH25 Kuaotonu River Bridge began on Sunday 22 Sep, following an early morning karakia by local Iwi. Our contractors have started to remove the wooden deck after stripping back the seal layers. The seal and deck have been removed by hand, and efforts made to protect the environment by installing a platform below the deck.

[image src="/assets/b241894b48/Kuaotunu-IMG_6039.jpg" id="1018" width="733" height="550" class="leftAlone ss-htmleditorfield-file image" title="Kuaotunu IMG 6039"]
properties.Name: Road Work: Kuaotunu River Bridge Repairs
